# PR-019: AGENTS.md layering and explicit Codex review trigger

Branch: pr-019-agents-codex

## Scope

Two changes:

1. Split AGENTS.md template into core (orchestrator-managed) and per-repo (user-managed) sections with clear boundaries.
2. Make daemon explicitly post `@codex review` after PR creation and after every fix push, instead of relying on Codex auto-trigger on push.

## Part 1: AGENTS.md layering

### templates/AGENTS.md

Restructure into clearly marked sections:

```
# AGENTS

## === ORCHESTRATOR CORE (do not edit) ===
[trigger phrases, runbooks, queue protocol, codex review gate,
 artifact generation, daemon mode, branch naming, queue stability]

## === PROJECT CONFIG (customize per repo) ===
[tech stack, repo invariants, MCP servers, secrets rules,
 CI specifics, test requirements]

## === LESSONS LEARNED (evolve over time) ===
[empty section with instructions: "Add rules here as you discover
 recurring patterns. Examples: 'Always add type hints', 'Wrap async
 calls in try/except', 'Codex frequently flags X - do Y proactively'"]
```

Orchestrator Core section: take current content from AGENTS.md, strip project-specific parts. Add a header comment: "This section is managed by Pipeline Orchestrator. Manual edits will be preserved but may be overwritten on orchestrator upgrades."

Project Config section: provide sensible defaults with TODO markers:
```
## Tech stack
- Language: Python 3.12  # TODO: update for your project
- Framework: # TODO
- Database: # TODO

## Repo invariants
# TODO: define your project's non-negotiable contracts

## MCP servers
Allowed MCP servers (by name):
- github (optional)
# TODO: add project-specific MCP servers

## Test requirements
- Tests required for every PR: true  # set to false for prototypes
- Minimum test coverage: none  # or specify percentage

## CI specifics
- Single entrypoint: scripts/ci.sh
# TODO: describe what your CI checks
```

Lessons Learned section: empty with guidance.

### src/daemon/scaffolder.py

No changes to scaffolder logic. It already copies templates/AGENTS.md as-is. The restructured template is the only change.

## Part 2: Explicit @codex review after every push

### src/daemon/runner.py

Modify handle_coding:
- After PR is found and state set to WATCH, add:
  ```python
  try:
      github_client.post_comment(self.owner_repo, candidate.number, "@codex review")
      self.log_event(f"Posted @codex review on PR #{candidate.number}")
  except Exception as exc:
      self.log_event(f"Warning: failed to post @codex review: {exc}")
  ```
- The post_comment failure is non-fatal: log warning, do not set ERROR. Review might still auto-trigger.

Modify handle_fix:
- After successful push (state set to WATCH), add same logic:
  ```python
  if self.state.current_pr is not None:
      try:
          github_client.post_comment(
              self.owner_repo, self.state.current_pr.number, "@codex review"
          )
          self.log_event(f"Posted @codex review on PR #{self.state.current_pr.number}")
      except Exception as exc:
          self.log_event(f"Warning: failed to post @codex review: {exc}")
  ```

handle_hung stays as-is (already posts @codex review).

### AGENTS.md (project root, this repo's own AGENTS.md)

Update Codex Review gate section to document the new behavior:
- Add note: "The daemon posts `@codex review` after PR creation and after every fix push. Codex Automatic Reviews should be configured for PR creation only (not every push) to avoid duplicate reviews."

### tests/test_runner.py (modify)

Add tests:
- handle_coding posts @codex review after PR found
- handle_coding succeeds even if post_comment fails (non-fatal)
- handle_fix posts @codex review after push
- handle_fix succeeds even if post_comment fails (non-fatal)

Mock github_client.post_comment.

## Success criteria
- scripts/ci.sh exits 0
- All tests pass
- templates/AGENTS.md has 3 clearly separated sections
- Daemon posts @codex review after every PR creation and fix push
- post_comment failure does not break the pipeline
